My approach

HR is not just administration. It is the architecture of how people work together. I build systems that run — and cultures that last. Two sides, one vision: people who belong, and operations that perform.

What I believe about HR
Recognition is underrated.
When someone feels genuinely valued, they give more than what's expected — not because they have to, but because they want to. I've seen this change people. Not just at work.
HR is not a department. It's the connection between people and purpose.
When it works well, you don't notice it. You just feel it — in how people show up, how they treat each other, how long they stay.
Every person you work with teaches you something.
Managing people isn't a one-way process. Every conversation, every difficult situation, every personality — it adds to how you see the world. I've learned as much from my team as they've learned from me.
Understanding the person comes before understanding the policy.
Rules and systems matter. But the best HR decisions I've made started with understanding what someone actually needed — not just what the handbook said.

How I recruit

In a high-volume call centre environment, I designed and ran a bi-weekly recruitment cycle delivering 10 qualified hires every two weeks — built from scratch and later scaled with a dedicated team.

1
Job Posting & CV Screening
Continuous job postings with structured CV screening — prioritising top candidates while ensuring every applicant is reviewed.
2
Initial Online Interview
Quick online screening to assess English proficiency, visa status, current availability, and relevant experience.
3
Team Manager Interview
Second-stage interview with the direct line manager to assess role fit, attitude, and team compatibility.
4
Offer Letter & Good Conduct Certificate
Approved candidates receive an offer letter and are requested to obtain a Good Conduct Certificate — processed in parallel to save time.
5
System Registration & Contract Preparation
Candidate details confirmed with the client for system access. Contracts, confidentiality agreements, and disciplinary policy documents prepared.
6
Day One Induction
New cohort signs documents, receives a company induction pack, takes an office tour, and is registered with SIO and Tamkeen (Bahrainis) or work visa processed (expats).
7
First Week Training + 90-Day Follow-Up
One week of structured training before going live. Weekly check-ins with the employee and their manager for 90 days to track integration and performance milestones.

Key projects & initiatives
01
HR System Built from Scratch
Grew Connect6 Group from 7 to 100+ employees in under 2 years. Built full HR infrastructure: policies, systems, government registrations, onboarding, and team structure — all from day one.
02
High-Volume Recruitment Pipeline
Designed and operated a bi-weekly recruitment cycle delivering 10 qualified hires every two weeks with a full 7-step process — built independently then scaled with a dedicated team.
03
Employee Engagement System
Monthly light activities, quarterly events, annual camping trip (families included), Ramadan gatherings, National Day, Women's Day, Pink October, and Employee of the Month programme.
04
Monthly Company Newsletter
Launched a monthly internal magazine covering company news, employee of the month, and birthdays — distributed across all group entities in Bahrain and Saudi Arabia.
05
University Internship Programme
Built ongoing partnerships with multiple universities to host interns year-round — developing fresh talent and creating a pipeline for future hiring.
06
KSA Cross-Border Operations
Manage Bahrain-side operations for Knooz Trading — including recruitment, marketing, compliance, supplier registration, trade show representation, and strategic partnerships.
